message:
refactor(agent): always emit ToolExecutionEnd for consistent lifecycle

Restructure the tool result event emission in execute_tool_calls() so
that ToolExecutionEnd is always emitted for every tool, regardless of
whether the tool actually executed or was skipped/aborted.

Previously, ToolExecutionEnd was only emitted inside the `!was_executed`
branch alongside the placeholder Update event. Tools that DID execute
relied on their own callback to emit End events, creating an asymmetry
where the lifecycle contract (Start→Update*→End) was split across two
different code paths.

Now:
- The `!was_executed` branch only emits a placeholder ToolExecutionUpdate
- ToolExecutionEnd is emitted unconditionally after the branch, ensuring
  every tool that received a ToolExecutionStart (from Phase 1) gets a
  matching End event regardless of execution path
- Comments updated to clarify the Phase 1 Start / Phase 2 Update+End
  lifecycle contract

This makes the event stream more predictable for consumers (TUI, RPC,
logging) that track tool lifecycle state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
